description: A plant with a long stem that trails or climbs by attaching to supports. 'Lowi' names the reaching wanderers—plants that find their way upward through others, winding toward light without standing alone.
sound_symbolism: The word winds and climbs. 'Lo' begins with liquid lengthening, the stem extending. Then 'wi' winds smoothly, the vine curving around its support.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo lowi' (the vine). Takes the vegetal classifier 'lipha' when counted. Includes grape vines, ivy, morning glory, and all climbing or trailing plants.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Vines were essential Art Nouveau motifs—the curving line, the organic spiral, growth that decorates as it climbs.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Vines teach interdependence—they rise by relationship, finding support in others rather than standing rigid and alone.
